text:
Athens Times

—

Social media found a new cult, competed in them in Paris, thanks to its eccentric dance figures. Australian breakdancing star who caused internet frenzy at the Olympics, is a university doctor who dealt with the sport after her husband introduced it to her. The 36-year-old Rachael Gunn, caused panic when she ascended to the stage during the first breakdancing competition, with movements compared to a child turning over and Homer Simpson running on the floor. Gunn introduced to the world as “Raygun” – the bgirl (“breakgirl”) her name – lost to competitors with half her age in qualifying rounds on Friday (09.08.2024), but her countdown with French Syssy attracted thousands of people. Gunn is, however, Australia’s top breakdancer – and special researcher in breaking. She works for the Macquarie University of Sydney as a creative arts researcher specializing in the “cultural policy of breakdancing” and has a PhD. Judge made the right call here because what was that move lol — MoNK BLOODY Ps (@MonkeyBlood) Along with Jeff Dunne – known as “J-Attack” when she was fighting – she raised Australia’s hopes of becoming the first Olympic champion in breakdancing. But her program – in some places she was banging on the floor and jumping like a kangaroo – confused viewers around the world and eventually left empty-handed, even though she showed technical skill and even made headstand. Australian Olympic breakdancer Rachael Gunn has a PhD in breakdancing and dance culture. “All my moves are original,” she told reporters after her performance. — Michael Nayna (@MikeNayna) If you don’t understand, Raygun didn’t make it to a point! “I could live my whole life and never think of anything as funny as Raygun, the 36-year-old Australian medalist breakdance,” wrote one user. There is no way I suck harder for the rest of 2024 than after watching a 36 year old Australian college professor named Raygun “breakdance” against teenagers — Your Best Friend’s Black Friend (@IamStatMatt) “I have never seen breaking, but I can already understand that Raygun has no hope in this event,” added another. “Raygun’s breakdancing looks like my five-year-old self going to my mom and saying ‘look what I can do’,” commented a third. “Congratulations to Raygun who had the guts to get out there,” noted another. And another summed up: “You appear as a lecturer with a PhD in breakdancing, with twice the age of all other contestants, score zero points and just run homer simpson around the floor while teenagers pin on you… love herrrr raygun you are everything”. But the 36-year-old probably didn’t let the haters influence her – in her post on Instagram she wrote: “Don’t be afraid to be different, get out there and represent yourself, you never know where that will lead you”.

Athens Times

News about Greece and the World.
title: Olympic Games: Panzurlism in social media with Australian breakdancing athlete who got zero – "figures that would make 5 years old" - Athens Times
author: uploader
